_comment: Recorded outputs keyed by case_id so `run.py --fixtures` scores against real observations. PC-001/002/003/005/007 are illustrative sample observations. PC-008 and PC-010 are derived from REAL flywheel-cycle-1 outcomes (2026-06-02): PC-008 = the dated reclaim window was closed with a sha256 manifest + closeout (a ~17G portion was escalated to the operator under the no-delete rule, NOT auto-deleted — 'verified' means real domain output was produced, not a fully-drained reclaim); PC-010 = a stale handoff was processed to a closure artifact with an operator-confirmed decision. PC-009 fails on purpose (the documented stale-memory gap). PC-004 (Notion refresh) and PC-006 (ambiguous directive) have NO recorded run and stay UNSCORED honestly.
